text: How important is a completely quiet environment for you to study effectively?
options: Absolutely essential — I cannot study without silence
Very important — I strongly prefer it
Somewhat important — I can manage with low noise
Not very important — background noise rarely bothers me
Not important at all — noise has no effect on me

text: How deeply can you concentrate when studying in a completely silent room?
options: Extremely deeply — I lose track of time entirely
Very deeply — I stay focused for long periods
Moderately — I focus well but take regular breaks
Lightly — my mind still wanders despite the silence
I struggle to concentrate even in total silence

text: How quickly does background noise (e.g. conversations, music) break your concentration?
options: I never notice it once I am focused
It takes a significant disturbance to break my focus
Moderate noise gradually pulls my attention away
Even low noise interrupts me within minutes
Any noise immediately destroys my concentration

text: How long can you maintain deep focus in your preferred study environment before losing concentration?
options: More than 90 minutes without any break
60 to 90 minutes before needing a short pause
30 to 60 minutes before my attention drifts
15 to 30 minutes before I lose focus
Less than 15 minutes before I become distracted

text: How often do you actively seek out a quiet zone (library, empty room) when you need to study seriously?
options: Always — it is part of my standard study routine
Often — I do so for most important study sessions
Sometimes — only when the task is particularly demanding
Rarely — I usually study wherever I happen to be
Never — I do not seek quiet zones at all

text: How does studying in a noisy environment affect the quality of your work?
options: It has no effect — my output is the same regardless
Slight effect — minor drop in quality under noise
Noticeable effect — I make more errors and work slower
Significant effect — the quality of my work drops sharply
Severe effect — I am practically unable to produce good work

text: How comfortable are you studying in shared spaces such as open libraries or common rooms?
options: Very comfortable — shared spaces work perfectly for me
Fairly comfortable — minor distractions are manageable
Somewhat uncomfortable — I tolerate it but prefer otherwise
Quite uncomfortable — shared spaces drain my concentration
Very uncomfortable — I avoid them entirely for study

text: How often do you use tools such as noise-cancelling headphones or earplugs to create a quiet study space?
options: Never — my environment is naturally quiet enough
Occasionally — only in unusually noisy situations
Sometimes — for medium-difficulty tasks
Often — I rely on them for most study sessions
Always — I cannot study at all without them

text: How well do you retain information studied in a quiet environment compared to a noisy one?
options: Equally well — the environment makes no difference
Slightly better in quiet — a small but noticeable difference
Moderately better — quiet clearly improves my retention
Much better — retention is significantly higher in silence
Dramatically better — I barely retain anything in noisy settings

text: How often do unexpected sounds (a door slamming, a phone ringing nearby) cause you to lose your train of thought?
options: Never — I remain focused regardless of sudden sounds
Rarely — only very loud or startling sounds affect me
Sometimes — moderate unexpected sounds break my focus
Often — even small sounds regularly disrupt my thinking
Always — any unexpected sound resets my concentration entirely

text: How would you describe your ability to enter a state of deep focus (flow) in your usual study setting?
options: Very easy — I enter a flow state quickly and regularly
Fairly easy — I usually get there within a few minutes
Moderate — it takes effort and the right conditions
Difficult — I rarely reach a genuine state of deep focus
Very difficult — I have almost never experienced deep focus

text: How much does low-level ambient sound (e.g. air conditioning, distant traffic) affect your ability to study?
options: Not at all — I find it neutral or even helpful
Very slightly — I notice it but it does not bother me
Somewhat — it is mildly distracting over time
Considerably — it noticeably reduces my concentration
Greatly — even soft ambient sound makes studying very hard

text: How often do you feel mentally exhausted after studying in a noisy or non-preferred environment?
options: Never — environmental noise does not tire me
Rarely — only after very long sessions in noisy places
Sometimes — moderate fatigue after a few hours
Often — I regularly feel drained after studying in noise
Always — even short noisy sessions leave me mentally depleted

text: How satisfied are you with the quiet study options currently available to you (at university, home, or elsewhere)?
options: Very satisfied — I always have access to an ideal quiet space
Fairly satisfied — adequate quiet spaces are usually available
Neutral — access is inconsistent but I manage
Fairly dissatisfied — quiet spaces are difficult to find
Very dissatisfied — I almost never have access to a quiet space

text: How effectively can you switch between different topics or tasks while remaining in deep concentration?
options: Very effectively — I transition smoothly without losing focus
Fairly effectively — brief adjustment time but focus is maintained
Moderately — switching topics disrupts my concentration noticeably
With difficulty — task-switching significantly breaks my focus
Very poorly — any switch essentially ends my concentration session

text: How often do you plan your study sessions around the availability of a quiet environment?
options: Never — I study whenever and wherever it is convenient
Rarely — only for major deadlines or exams
Sometimes — I consider it for important tasks
Often — quiet availability is a key factor in my planning
Always — I will not begin a study session without securing a quiet space

text: How does studying with background music (without lyrics) affect your concentration?
options: It helps me focus — I concentrate better with instrumental music
It is neutral — no noticeable positive or negative effect
It mildly distracts me — I prefer silence over music
It noticeably distracts me — music reduces my concentration
It severely distracts me — any music makes studying very hard

text: How well do you perform on complex cognitive tasks (problem-solving, essay writing) in your usual study environment?
options: Excellently — I consistently produce high-quality work
Well — my performance is generally strong with minor lapses
Adequately — I complete tasks but not always to my best ability
Poorly — I frequently struggle to think clearly during tasks
Very poorly — my cognitive performance is consistently low

text: How aware are you of other people's movements or activities around you while studying?
options: Not aware at all — I am fully absorbed in my work
Slightly aware — I notice them but am not distracted
Moderately aware — they occasionally pull my attention
Very aware — I frequently lose focus due to others nearby
Extremely aware — I cannot ignore what others around me are doing

text: How confident are you that your current study environment supports your best academic performance?
options: Very confident — my environment is ideal for my needs
Fairly confident — it meets most of my requirements
Uncertain — it is acceptable but far from ideal
Not very confident — my environment often works against me
Not confident at all — my environment significantly hinders my performance
